pages.raw_text:
A letter from Thomas Cooper, man.
ager of the Northern Pacific has been
received by Corporation Counsel Jud-
son and filed with the city clerk in
which Mr.. Cooper requests that no
further action be taken by the city in
the matter of the street crossing at
Division street and Sprague avenue I'M
til the matter has been taken up with
other officials Of the Northern Pacific
and their action is made known to the
city officials. "If the situation is not
satisfactory" the letter states, then
the corporation counsel is at liberty
to again take up the matter with the
railroad officials to find some remedy


Mr.. Cooper says he has advised the
railroad officials to have that part of
Division street paved abutting on the
freight depot and has personally taken
the matter up with the president.


In the matter of the Brownies addi-
ton crossing Mr.. Cooper has left the
matter in the hands of the company's
Spokane counsel, John NJ. Bum. That
no misunderstanding might arise be-
tween the city and the railroad com
pany as to their verbal contract, the
city clerk has put the contract in writ
ing for the use Of present and future
officials
